title: URL parser divergence: hand-rolled parse_relay_addr vs tokio-socks internals
description:
parse_relay_addr() (lines 420-439) and parse_proxy_addr() (lines 402-415) duplicate URL parsing logic that tokio-socks already performs. Risk: A future tokio-socks version might handle edge cases differently (IPv6 literals, non-ASCII domains, query strings). A contributor might update one path but not the other, silently accepting URLs that fail at the SOCKS5 layer with no user feedback until runtime.

Recommendation: 
1. Add unit tests that validate parse_relay_addr/parse_proxy_addr against actual tokio-socks behavior (edge cases: IPv6 address literals, domains with underscores, ports out of range)
2. Document in code why we can't defer parsing to tokio-socks (if there's a reason) or consider refactoring to rely on tokio-socks parsing where possible
